headline: New Hilton and Marriott credit card offers — earn up to 175,000 points
summary: American Express is offering eligible new cardholders welcome bonuses of up to 175,000 points on Hilton and Marriott credit cards for a limited amount of time.

raw.headline: New Hilton and Marriott credit card offers — earn up to 175,000 points
raw.summary: American Express is offering eligible new cardholders welcome bonuses of up to 175,000 points on Hilton and Marriott credit cards for a limited amount of time.

headline: Jamal Satli Iglesias, Marriott International and Aimbridge Hospitality Announce Landmark All-Inclusive Resort in Riviera Maya
summary: RIVIERA MAYA, Mexico, May 21, 2026--Jamal Satli Iglesias, President of real estate investment fund Grupo Satli, together with Marriott International and Aimbridge Hospitality, today announced the launch of an international project in Riviera Maya (Mexico), further strengthening the positioning of the three companies in the all-inclusive resort segment.

headline: Select Group expands UK hospitality platform with three golf resorts
summary: All three newly acquired properties were rebranded to Delta Hotels by Marriott in 2023.

raw.headline: Select Group expands UK hospitality platform with three golf resorts
raw.summary: All three newly acquired properties were rebranded to Delta Hotels by Marriott in 2023.

headline: MAYFAIR GOLD ADVANCES DISTRICT-SCALE EXPLORATION STRATEGY
summary: Mayfair Gold Corp. ("Mayfair", "Mayfair Gold", or the "Company") (TSXV: MFG) (NYSE American: MINE) is pleased to announce that it is initiating a regional exploration program across its Timmins camp land package, following the acquisition of the Guibord, Marriott, and Holloway properties and the continued advancement of exploration at the South Block. These properties complement the Company's flagship Fenn-Gib Gold Project, which hosts a 4.3 million ounce indicated mineral resource of gold (181.

headline: Despite Iran war, hotel demand is now back on track, says Marriott APAC President
summary: Travelers from abroad cancelled hotel bookings in Asia-Pacific after the Iran war started, says Rajeev Menon, Marriott International's APAC President (excluding China). But demand is back on track, as more APAC travelers book trips in Asia this summer.
